lines.en_lit: MISHNA: “And he shall go out to the altar that is before the Lord This is the golden altar, He began to cleanse downward. From where does he begin? from the northeast corner, northwest southwest southeast place he begins for a sin-offering on the outer altar, there he finishes on the inner altar.
Rabbi Eliezer says: he stood in place and sprinkled

And on all the corners he would present from below upward, except for that that was before him, which he would present from above downward. He sprinkled on pure of the altar seven times And he would pour the remainder of the blood on the western base of the outer altar. he would pour on the outer altar, on its southern base. These and those mixed in the canal and flow out to the Kidron River. sold to gardeners for fertilizer. misuse

GEMARA: The Sages taught: “And he shall go out to the altar” What the verse states Rabbi Neḥemya said: because we find with regard to the bull brought for all the mitzvot, that when the priest sprinkles he stands past the altar and sprinkles toward the curtain; might that here, too, the same
the verse states: “And he shall go out to the altar.” Where was he on the inner side of the altar,

It was taught another before the Lord What the verse states: Rabbi Neḥemya said: because we find with regard to the bull and the goat of Yom Kippur that when he sprinkles Priest stands on the inner side of the altar, and sprinkles toward the curtain; might here, too, the same

the verse states: altar of sweet incense before the Lord, which is in the Tent of Meeting” altar before the Lord, but the priest is not before the Lord. How so? stands on the outer side of the altar and sprinkles.

He began to cleanse downward. The Sages taught He began to cleanse downward. From where would he begin? from the southeast corner southwest northwest northeast the statement of Rabbi Akiva. Rabbi Yosei HaGelili says: from the northeast corner, northwest southwest southeast
begins Rabbi Yosei HaGelili, there Rabbi Akiva ends; begins Rabbi Akiva, there Rabbi Yosei HaGelili ends.

Everyone in any case, he does not perform at the corner that he encounters first. What is the reason Shmuel said: that the verse states: “And he shall go out to the altar,” until he goes out from entire altar.
And Rabbi Akiva, let him go around via the right. Let us say they disagree with regard to of Rami bar Yeḥezkel.

As Rami bar Yeḥezkel said: the sea, that Solomon built, “It stood upon twelve oxen, three looking toward the north, and three looking toward the west, and three looking toward the south, and three looking toward the east; and the sea was set upon them above, and all their hinder parts were inward” you learn that all turns that you turn should be only to the right to the east this Sage, is of of Rami bar Yeḥezkel, and this Sage, is of not Rami bar Yeḥezkel.
No, everyone is of Rami bar Yeḥezkel, but here they disagree with regard to this One Sage, holds: We derive inside from outside: And one Sage, holds: We do not derive inside from outside.

And Rabbi Akiva, although he does not derive inside from outside, if wants, let him perform this if he wants, let him perform that Rabbi Akiva said to you: by right he should begin by that corner that he reaches first, as Reish Lakish said: One does not pass on mitzvot.

And why does he not do due to which is written: “He shall go out to the altar,” until he goes out from entire altar. And since he presents at that corner he then comes to that corner where he should have presented first.
